seoTitle: How to Send Automated Reminder Emails in Power Automate
metaDescription: Build a Power Automate flow that sends personalized Outlook reminder emails for deadlines, approvals, and tasks from Excel or SharePoint data.
intro: Chasing overdue tasks and approvals manually is inefficient once a team grows past a handful of people. This guide shows how to build a Power Automate flow that automatically sends reminder emails through Outlook, using data pulled from Excel, SharePoint, or Microsoft Lists.

steps.heading: Create a reminder flow
steps.body: Start a new cloud flow dedicated to sending reminders, separate from other automations so it's easy to manage and update.

steps.heading: Configure the trigger and data source
steps.body: Set the flow to run on a schedule or in response to an event, then connect it to Excel, SharePoint, or Microsoft Lists to pull the records that need a reminder.

steps.heading: Build the reminder email
steps.body: Add an Outlook action and use dynamic content from the data source to personalize each email with the recipient's name and relevant task details.

steps.heading: Test and schedule the workflow
steps.body: Run the flow with sample data to confirm the reminders send correctly, then set the schedule so it runs automatically going forward.

faq.question: Can reminder emails be personalized for each recipient?
faq.answer: Yes, dynamic content from your Excel, SharePoint, or Microsoft Lists data source lets you insert names and task-specific details into each email.

faq.question: How do I stop the flow from sending duplicate reminders?
faq.answer: Add a condition that checks whether a reminder has already been sent for that item, such as a status field, before sending another one.

faq.question: What triggers work for this kind of reminder flow?
faq.answer: Both scheduled recurrence triggers and event-based triggers, like a new item being added, can work depending on whether reminders are time-based or tied to specific events.

faq.question: Can this be used for invoice or approval reminders?
faq.answer: Yes, this same approach applies to deadlines, overdue tasks, approvals, meetings, and invoices, since it's built around whatever data source you connect.
